channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Global geopolitical news curated from Tier 1 and Tier 2 sources

item.title: 🌏 Asia's Energy Pain Far From Over as LNG Shock Hits World's Factory
item.description: Asian economies are grappling with tightened LNG supply, mounting foreign exchange pressure and damage already locked into Q2 performance as the Iran war disrupts 20% of global oil and LNG flows. The Philippines' GDP growth was downgraded to 3.7% from projected 5-6%, with the IMF warning of 'permanent scarring' across the region.

item.title: 🇮🇳 India Faces Fertiliser Crisis as Iran War Disrupts Gulf Supplies
item.description: India's urea imports have surged to $935 per tonne from $508 pre-war, with ammonia facilities in Qatar and Saudi Arabia shut after Iranian strikes. Domestic urea production has fallen from 2.5 million tonnes monthly to just 1.5 million tonnes due to LNG supply disruptions, threatening the kharif crop season.

item.title: 🇦🇺 Australia's Big Banks Brace for Oil Shock Fallout as Hormuz Remains Blocked
item.description: Australia's major lenders Westpac and National Australia Bank are increasing bad loan provisions as the prolonged Hormuz closure begins impacting the economy. The government has announced a $1 billion emergency loan program administered through the big four banks to help businesses weather the fuel crisis.

item.title: 🇰🇵 North Korea Tests Cluster Warhead Missiles With Kim Jong Un Observing
item.description: North Korean leader Kim Jong Un supervised test-launches of the upgraded Hwasongpho-11 Ra tactical ballistic missile with cluster bomb and fragmentation mine warheads. Five missiles struck a 12.5-13 hectare target area with 'very high density,' demonstrating the regime's continued weapons development amid global distractions.

item.title: 🇪🇸🇪🇺 Spain Urges EU to End Association Agreement With Israel Over International Law Violations
item.description: Spain announced it will ask the European Union to end its association agreement with Israel over alleged violations of international law. The move marks a significant escalation in European diplomatic pressure on Israel amid the broader Middle East conflict.

item.title: 🇯🇵🇨🇳 Japan's Taiwan Strait Warship Transit on Treaty Anniversary Raises New Flashpoint With China
item.description: A Japanese warship's transit of the Taiwan Strait on the anniversary of a key security treaty drew sharp criticism from Beijing, which said the passage 'harmed the feelings of the Chinese people.' Tensions in East Asia reached a fresh peak amid competing maritime claims.

item.title: 🇯🇵🇵🇭 Japan Self-Defense Forces to Join Philippine-US Military Drills in South China Sea
item.description: Japan's Self-Defense Forces will take part in annual military drills led by the Philippines and the US, testing Tokyo's commitment to security in the South China Sea. The exercises come as Japan expands its regional defense partnerships amid growing tensions with China.

item.title: 🇧🇭 Bahrain Reviews Citizenship Laws After Foiling Iran-Linked Cells
item.description: Bahrain's King Hamad announced measures against those who 'betrayed' the country after authorities foiled Iran-linked cells. The Gulf state is reviewing citizenship laws, potentially stripping nationality from dissidents, amid heightened regional tensions with Iran.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇲🇽 Two US Embassy Officials Die in Car Crash in Mexico After Drug Operation
item.description: Two US embassy officials died in a car crash in Chihuahua alongside two Mexican agents following a drug operation. The officials were trainers working with Mexican counterparts as part of ongoing bilateral security cooperation efforts.

item.title: 🚢 US Navy Seizes Iranian Cargo Ship Near Strait of Hormuz, Tehran Vows Response
item.description: The US Navy fired on and seized an Iranian-flagged cargo ship near the Strait of Hormuz after a six-hour standoff, with Iran's military calling it 'piracy' and vowing a swift response. The incident throws the fragile ceasefire into question as US negotiators head to Pakistan for talks.

item.title: ☢️ CBS Reports on Operation to Remove Iran's Highly Enriched Uranium Stockpile
item.description: A 60 Minutes report examines the challenges of securing Iran's estimated 1,000 pounds of 60%-enriched uranium stored deep underground at Isfahan. Experts say the operation would require thousands of US troops and weeks on the ground, with any deal needing Iran's cooperation.

item.title: 🛑 Iran Closes Strait of Hormuz Again, Says Traffic Control Until War Ends
item.description: Iran has again closed the Strait of Hormuz, reversing a brief reopening and saying it will maintain 'supervision and control' over traffic until the war definitively ends. Iran's chief negotiator stated: 'It is impossible for others to pass through the Strait of Hormuz while we cannot.'

item.title: 🇧🇾 Belarus Leader Says Meeting With Trump Possible Once 'Big Deal' Is Ready
item.description: Belarusian President Alexander Lukashenko said he would be ready to meet US President Donald Trump once a 'big deal' between the two countries has been prepared. The statement signals potential shifts in Belarus-West relations amid the ongoing geopolitical realignment.

item.title: 🏦 IMF and World Bank Meetings Show Limits in Mitigating Shocks, Reliance on US
item.description: The IMF spring meetings revealed growing global anxiety about a 13 million barrel per day oil shortfall, but US officials treated the energy crisis as a sideshow. The disconnect highlights how counting on US leadership to resolve crises is 'no longer the guarantee it had long been.'

item.title: 🚀 Iran Replenishing Missile Launchers Faster Than Before War, Guards Commander Says
item.description: Iran's Revolutionary Guards Aerospace Force commander said the country now updates and replenishes its missile and drone launchers at a higher speed than before the war with the US and Israel. The statement signals Iran's continued military capacity despite weeks of strikes.

item.title: ⛪ Pope Leo Decries Escalation in Ukraine War, Calls for Weapons to Fall Silent
item.description: Pope Leo on Sunday decried the intensification of the war in Ukraine, calling 'for the weapons to fall silent and for the path of dialogue to be followed.' The appeal comes amid Russia's recent mass missile attacks that killed at least 17 civilians.

item.title: 🛡️ Ukraine Pushes Europe to Build Ballistic Missile Defense System
item.description: President Zelenskiy said Europe must have its own defense system against ballistic weapons, with Ukraine already holding talks with several countries on its creation. The push comes as ballistic missile defense remains one of Ukraine's biggest challenges in the war with Russia.

item.title: 🇧🇬 Pro-Russian Former President Set for Landslide Win in Bulgaria Election
item.description: Pro-Russian former President Rumen Radev is set for a runaway victory in Bulgaria's election and may even secure a parliamentary majority, exit polls showed. The result could end years of weak coalition governments and alter the EU member's foreign policy orientation.

item.title: 🇫🇷🇬🇷 France and Greece to Renew Defense Pact for Five Years Amid Iran War
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron will travel to Greece next week to renew a security pact between the two countries for another five years, a Greek government official said. The renewal comes against the backdrop of the war in Iran and shifting European security dynamics.

item.title: ⛪ Israeli Soldier Filmed Smashing Jesus Statue in Lebanon Sparks Outrage
item.description: An image showing an Israeli soldier using a jackhammer to smash a statue of Jesus in southern Lebanon went viral, sparking outrage including among US conservatives. The incident in the Maronite Christian village of Debel drew condemnation from figures like Marjorie Taylor Greene and Matt Gaetz, potentially damaging Israel's support among evangelical Christians.

item.title: 🚢 Iran Vows 'Swift Response' After US Seizes Iranian Cargo Ship Near Hormuz
item.description: Iran's military vowed retaliation after US Marines seized an Iranian-flagged cargo ship near the Strait of Hormuz. Trump said the Navy 'blew a hole in the engineroom' of the vessel Touska. Meanwhile, Brent crude surged 7% to $96.85 as markets reacted to renewed Hormuz uncertainty.

item.title: 🤖 Chinese Humanoid Robot Beats Human Record in Half-Marathon by 6 Minutes
item.description: A humanoid robot named Lightning, developed by Chinese company Honor, completed a half-marathon in Beijing in 50 minutes 26 seconds—beating the human world record by over 6 minutes. The race attracted 105 robot competitors, up from 21 last year, showcasing China's rapid advances in humanoid robotics and autonomous navigation.

item.title: ⚖️ Iranian National Charged in US With Trafficking Arms to Sudan for Tehran
item.description: US federal prosecutors charged Iranian national Shamim Mafi with brokering the sale of drones, bombs, and millions of rounds of ammunition manufactured by Iran and sold to Sudan. Mafi, a lawful US resident since 2016, was arrested at Los Angeles International Airport and faces up to 20 years in prison if convicted.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukraine's Long-Range Strikes Inflicted $2.3 Billion in Russian Oil Losses in March
item.description: Ukrainian estimates show long-range strikes on Russian oil infrastructure caused at least $2.3 billion in lost oil revenue for Russia in March alone, Zelensky said. The attacks disabled about 40% of Russia's oil export capacity as Ukraine targets the Kremlin's key revenue source.

item.title: 📉 Gulf Equities Mixed as Hormuz Uncertainty Caps Ceasefire Optimism
item.description: Gulf equities ended mixed on Sunday after President Trump said Iran had committed a serious violation of the ceasefire, renewing uncertainty over the Strait of Hormuz and the state of US-Iran talks despite his insistence that a peace deal would happen. Markets remain cautious amid conflicting signals.
